vulnerabilities.name: multer-no-file-filter
vulnerabilities.message: multer() configured without `fileFilter` and/or `limits`. Reject unexpected MIME types and cap size to mitigate upload-driven RCE and resource exhaustion.

vulnerabilities.name: jwt-no-expiry
vulnerabilities.message: JWT token created without expiration (expiresIn). Tokens without expiry remain valid forever — if stolen, they can never be invalidated. Always set a short expiration: { expiresIn: "15m" }.

vulnerabilities.name: mass-assignment-is-admin-js
vulnerabilities.message: Mass assignment / privilege escalation: req.body.is_admin (or role/isAdmin) is read directly into the persistence layer. An attacker can self-elevate by including the field in any registration / profile-update request. Whitelist mutable fields explicitly.

vulnerabilities.name: plaintext-password-storage-js
vulnerabilities.message: Possible plaintext password storage: a `password` field is written to the database without an obvious hash step (bcrypt / argon2 / scrypt). Hash with bcrypt(cost ≥ 12) or argon2id before persisting.

vulnerabilities.name: xss-res-send-concat
vulnerabilities.message: Reflected XSS: req.* data inserted into HTML response via concatenation / template literals without HTML-encoding. Use a templating engine with auto-escaping or sanitize via `escape-html`.

vulnerabilities.name: no-new-privileges
vulnerabilities.message: Service 'db' allows for privilege escalation via setuid or setgid binaries. Add 'no-new-privileges:true' in 'security_opt' to prevent this.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-732: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
